explanation: TRUE POSITIVE confirmed. The claimReward() function has two critical flaws: (1) the return value of verify() is never checked (line 835), making the entire signature authorization system dead code — any caller with any inputs can claim arbitrary rewards; and (2) even if the return value were checked, there is no nonce tracking mapping in the contract, so any valid signature could be replayed indefinitely. Both issues are confirmed by direct code inspection with no mitigating controls found anywhere in the contract.
